name: Plastic Hunting - Secondary Schools & Students (Netherlands)
description:
Plastic Hunting begins with a warm welcome from our Plastic Whale Rangers. Students learn about plastic pollution in a way that feels empowering rather than overwhelming — focusing on solutions and shared responsibility.

Then we head outside.

Working in teams, students explore their own neighbourhood and collect litter using grabbers and gloves. What starts as a cleanup quickly becomes something more: collaboration, leadership and awareness in action.

Students divide roles, encourage one another and discover that teamwork makes a visible difference.

To keep the energy playful and
highlights: Professional Plastic Whale Rangers

name: Korean Zaanse Schans
description:
Discover the most iconic symbols of the Netherlands!
You know that photo you absolutely have to take when you travel somewhere Well, that’s it you haven’t really been to the Netherlands if you don’t have a picture with its famous windmills in the background. This tour is short but intense, designed so you can make the most of your time. In just one afternoon you’ll see different types of windmills, learn how traditional wooden clogs are made, and enjoy a tasting of delicious Dutch cheese. Sounds like a great plan, right?

This excursion takes place on a private bus, because there’s simply no better way to enjoy an afternoon in the Netherlands.

Qué está incluido

Includes transportation by bus from Amsterdam and a Korean speaking guide throughout the entire tour.

Itinerario

Windmills

name: Evening (Sunset) - Amsterdam Walking Tour
description:
Arriving in Amsterdam later in the day?

Perfect. The city is just warming up.

Join us for a relaxed 2-hour evening walk through the historic centre, when the canals glow, the streets soften, and Amsterdam shows its slightly more mysterious side.

You’ll explore the same iconic route as our beloved Hello Amsterdam tour — but in a different mood.

Golden lights on the canals. Quieter streets. Locals heading out for dinner and drinks.

And somewhere between the stories, the laughter, and the first night breeze along the canals…

Amsterdam will start to make sense.

Itinerary

Stop 1 – Dam Square: A warm welcome in the city’s birthplace. Surrounded by the Royal Palace, the Nieuwe Kerk, and the National Monument, your guide sets the stage for the adventure ahead. This square is Amsterdam’s living room — the place where trade, tolerance, and transf

This wonderful tour covers two magnificent Dutch highlights in one day! Enjoy a visit to the Dutch heritage site of De Zaanse Schans. After the Zaanse Schans you will visit the ‘Little Venice’ of the Netherlands: Giethoorn, only accessible by boat and with an absence of roads and cars. In Giethoorn you'll join us for a private boat tour.

description:
Welcome aboard the Pure Boats morning cruise!

Begin your day by joining Pure Boats on the Good morning Amsterdam cruise.

A morning Amsterdam canal cruise is more than special and is perhaps our favourite time to cruise. Most people don’t immediately consider waking up to board a canal cruise, which is what makes this experience all the more exclusive!

Ideally suited for families with kids of all ages and small groups of friends.

name: E-Bike Sightseeing Tour
description:
Get an intimate glimpse of the Netherlands' cycle-loving capital on this electric bike tour.

Stay clear of the crowds as you cruise through areas such as Jordaan and Vondelpark, and benefit from your small-group guide's personalised attention and insights as you visit landmarks including the Anne Frank House and Brouwerij 't IJ.

Experience Amsterdam like a local on a city bike tour and benefit from the personalised attention of your small-group guide.

Tick off more sights on two wheels than possible on foot.

The e-bike comes covered in the price.

What's included

• Explore Amsterdam by electric bike, and never get tired
• See the main highlights and hidden squares
• Cruise along the canals of Amsterdam
• Guided by a local guide

Cancellations

name: Excursão de Amsterdam até aos moinhos de vento de Zaanse Schans
description:
Partindo do centro de Amsterdam, esta excursão nos levará até o povoado de Zaanse Schans, famoso mundialmente por seus antigos moinhos de vento perfeitamente conservados. Você terá a oportunidade de conhecer por dentro um desses moinhos, símbolo do país, e verá como essas estruturas ajudaram e continuam ajudando na produção de produtos como óleos, grãos e mostarda. Para mergulhar nas tradições holandesas, iremos visitar uma fábrica de queijos e degustar esse delicioso produto regional, além de ver como se fabrica o tamanco de madeira holandês. Depois de 3 horas imersos no estilo de vida rural holandês, voltaremos ao centro de Amsterdam.

O que está incluído

Guia em português.
Bilhete para o transporte entre Amsterdam e região dos moinhos.
Visita aos Moinhos de vento de Zaanse Schans.
Visita a uma fábrica de queijo.
Demonstração de fabricação de tamancos de madeira.

name: Bill's Private Bike Tour Amsterdam
description:
Hi Friends! A very warm welcome. Want Bill all to yourself? You can book Bill privately for your group or family and get his undivided attention! Bill's Private Bike Tour takes you through Bill's neighborhood, revealing many secrets of Amsterdam and Dutch culture.

During Bill's Private Bike Tour you will see parts of Amsterdam most tourists never get to see! We will stay outside the city center and explore the city from a local perspective. On Bill’s Bike Tour you will hardly interact with other traffic as Bill has selected a beautiful and safe route through the back streets of Amsterdam.

Bill lives right around the corner from the meeting point and he will tell you everything about his neighborhood. Before setting off we will take 10 minutes to make sure you are comfortable on your bike and Bill will explain basic cycling rules. From then on it is nonstop fun, cruising on a typical Dutch bike!

What's included

Live guide in English by a local neighborhood hero

Quality bikes in all sizes, including tandem and carrier bikes

Professional photos and videos of the tour

A typical Dutch treat: Tony's Chocolonely or a stroopwafel

Tip and tricks for your stay in Amste

name: Privé Amsterdamse Stadswandeling
description:
De Yellow Bike Wandeling neemt je mee langs de mooi verlichte grachten en winkelstraatjes die eeuwen geschiedenis ademen.

Bezoek de Negen Straatjes, aanschouw de prachtige Westertoren en leer over de recente geschiedenis van het Spui, waar de provo’s hun happenings hielden en opriepen tot burgerlijk verzet. Uiteraard kom je langs het Anne Frank Huis en de voormalige arbeiderswijk Jordaan.

In de Spuistraat komt de Amsterdamse kraakgeschiedenis aan bod. Geniet van Amsterdam op zijn mooist en ontdek deze prachtige stad in beweging.

name: Puff & Pencil Copenhagen Skirt - Wrap Skirt Workshop (Amsterdam West)
description:
The COPENHAGEN skirt is a low-waisted wrap skirt with two ties at the front side. The skirt features a wide waistband and an asymmetric front overlap. It fastens with a combination of a hidden button on one side and the two ties on the other side. The skirt is without lining.

Join designer Michelle during this 4h workshop to create the Copenhagen Skirt from Puff & Pencil.

name: Family Friendly Rijksmuseum Guided Tour - Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Step into the world of Rembrandt, Vermeer, and Dutch history on this 2.5-hour private tour of the Rijksmuseum, one of the most fascinating museums in Europe. With a family-friendly guide leading the way, this tour makes the world of art and history fun, interactive, and easy to enjoy for kids and adults alike.

Explore the museum’s highlights, starting with Rembrandt’s The Night Watch—an enormous and dramatic painting full of mystery and movement. Learn how artists like Vermeer used light and color in magical ways, and admire The Milkmaid, one of his most famous and peaceful scenes. Take a closer look at incredibly detailed dollhouses, discover ornate weapons and armor, and even peek inside the museum’s grand 19th-century library—it looks straight out of a storybook! Along the way, your guide will share fun stories, quirky facts, and surprising details that bring the Dutch Golden Age to life.

Perfect for families looking to explore art in a way t
highlights:
The Milkmaid by Johannes Vermeer
Admire Vermeer’s luminous depiction of a simple domestic scene, where meticulous detail and radiant light transform an ordinary moment into a timeless masterpiece.

The Cuypers Library
Step into the Rijksmuseum’s breathtaking 19th-century library, the oldest art historical library in the Netherlands and admire its towering bookcases and spiral staircases.
Please arrive 10 minutes before the scheduled start time.
Your guide will be wearing a Babylon badge on a Babylon lanyard. They will also reach out to you on the morning of the tour to confirm the meeting point.

name: Amsterdam Highlights Bike Tour
description:
Join a friendly 2,5-hour guided bike tour in Amsterdam led by a passionate local guide and explore the city’s most iconic landmarks and hidden gems. From the historic Jordaan district to the famous canals of the UNESCO-listed city center, ride past Anne Frank House, the Van Gogh museum, leafy parks, and charming streets far from the crowds.

Cruise comfortably on a premium Dutch bike as your guide shares fascinating stories, local anecdotes, and insider tips about Amsterdam’s history, culture, and everyday life. Designed as a relaxed, small-group experience, this guided bike tour is ideal for first-time visitors looking to discover Amsterdam in an authentic and enjoyable way, beyond the usual tourist paths.

Whether you’re traveling solo, with friends, as a couple, or with family, this Amsterdam guided bike tour offers the perfect balance of culture, nature, and local atmosphere, the best way to experience the city like a true local.

name: Wheelchair-Friendly Amsterdam Private Tour
description:
Discover Amsterdam comfortably on a private tour tailored for guests with limited mobility and wheelchair users. Your guide meets you in your hotel lobby and escorts you to an accessible vehicle for a thoughtful journey through the city’s highlights, including the Canal Belt, Museumplein and Vondelpark. The route avoids steep bridges and difficult cobbles where possible, with flexible stops for photos and short visits. The tour combines local storytelling and relaxed pacing so you can enjoy Amsterdam’s history, architecture and atmosphere with confidence and ease.

What's included

Private guided tour of Amsterdam

Professional local Payne Hurds guide

Hotel lobby pic

name: Visit the Girl with the Pearl Earring, The Hague & Delft City
description:
Visit the Girl with the Pearl Earring, The Hague & Delft City

Take a journey through Dutch history on this private tour of The Hague & Delft City. Meet the Girl with the Pearl Earring at the Mauritshuis Museum and walk the cities of The Hague & Delft.

This magnificent tour highlights the historical, political and royal heart of The Netherlands. The Hague (Den Haag) is a city full of history, fine museums and art galleries. The Mauritshuis Museum is world famous and has more than two hundred works of art on display from Dutch and Flemish masters. Delft, a canal-ringed city enjoys a worldwide reputation due to its connection with Johannes Vermeer, Delft Blue earthenware and the Royal House of Orange.

name: History of Amsterdam - City Center Guided Walking Tour - Private (FRENCH)
description:
Start your visit to Amsterdam by diving into its rich history and learn how a small 12th century fishing village with a dam became one of the most cosmopolitan of capital cities in the world. Discover the streets and sights of Amsterdam on this 2.5 hour Private guided walking tour. It’s perfect for a first-time visitor to get oriented and introduced properly.

Our guides will crisscross its iconic canals and bridges while ducking into its quiet courtyards, offering postcard images for you and an intimate group of travelers. Discover its leaning houses and other landmarks while learning how to navigate the snaking streets of the city center and the banks of the Amstel River. Our local guides will eagerly share their insider knowledge so that you can maximize your time in Amsterdam.

Cancellations

Child - €49 (9 and under)

name: Combo: Rijksmuseum + Amsterdam History - City Center Guided Tour - Private (SPANISH)
description:
For those who aren’t familiar with Dutch culture – and who is really? – we will explore its rich and vibrant past on this popular combo tour. It’s the perfect way to mix culture, history, and sightseeing while spending the day with one of our vibrant young guides. The 5 to 5.5 hour private tour will include a visit to the Rijksmuseum followed by a historic walking tour of the city center. Your guide will provide you with a ticket that allows you to skip the line at the museum.

Begin at Holland’s answer to the Louvre, called the Rijksmuseum. Explore Dutch history through a selection over 8000 objects on display. We’ll discuss familiar names like Rembrandt as well as lesser known items like 17th century dollhouses. Understand the meaning behind Vermeer’s “The Milkmaid” and marvel at the expansive 19th century library. At the end of the tour, you’ll have a good overview of the museum’s collection and you may even know how to pronounce its name correctly.

After a break for lunch, move outdoors and orient yourself to Amsterdam with an introduction to its history and learn how a small 12th century fishing village with a simple dam became one of the most popular capital cities in the world. Our guides will crisscross its iconic canals and bridges while peeking into its quiet courtyards, offering a postcard image of the city. You will discover its leaning houses and other landmarks while learning how to navigate the oftentimes tricky streets of the city center. Our local guides are also eager to share their insider knowledge so that you can maximize your time spent in Amsterdam.

Cancellations
highlights: Youth - €139 (Ages 10 - 17)
Child - €105 (9 and under)
5 - 5.5 hours (including a lunch break)
Meet your guide outside the Cobra Cafe by the Toilet/Parking entrance on the north side of the building facing the Rijksmuseum. (between the bicycle lane and the Cobra Cafe)

name: Excursión Privada Jardines Keukenhof
description:
Duración

5 Horas

Acerca

Conoce de forma más exclusiva los jardines de Keukenhof con uno de nuestros guías hispanohablantes, partiendo desde Ámsterdam con un trayecto de tan sólo 40 minutos. Cuenta con una extensión de más de 32 hectáreas, siendo la exposición floral más grande del mundo. Una vez en Keukenhof, uno podrá deleitarse con impresionantes paisajes adornados con la flor más representativa de los holandeses, el tulipán, además de otras variedades como jacintos, orquídeas y narcisos, entre otras.

Durante el trayecto, el guía llevará a cabo una contextualización histórica para comprender la estrecha relación entre el tulipán y los Países Bajos, pasando al mismo tiempo por paisajes únicos de la geografía neerlandesa. Una vez en el parque, se realizará una visita guiada de aproximadamente 45 minutos.

Al tratarse de una visita privada, el cliente podrá disfrutar de tiempo libre para hacer fotos o realizar compras. El itinerario podrá pactarse al comienzo del tour para adaptarlo a los deseos del cliente, siempre considerando la duración del mismo.

name: Public: World War II and the Holocaust in Amsterdam
description:
€40 p.p |3 hrs | Ages 13+ | Off the beaten path!

	1198 Reviewers
#43 of 1,340 Tours in Amsterdam
TripAdvisor Traveler Rating
According to TripAdvisor travelers as of April 2026
Public tour: History Walks Amsterdam
Rates

Adult (13+): €40

Duration

3 hours

About

See important sites and monuments relating to the Nazi occupation of Amsterdam (1940-45). Let us be your guides and inform you about this traumatic episode in the Dutch capital's long history. The tour is ideal for visitors who like to go off the beaten path and want to learn more about this important aspect of the city's past.

For those with a passion for history, this is a tour not to be missed.

The tours are given by three retired historians with a passion for the history of the German occupation during the Second World War.

We do not visit The Anne Frank House, the Resistance Museum or the Hollandsche Schouwburg (the deportation center). Tickets for these museums and drinks are not included.

About halfway through the walk, a short break will be provided for a coffee and/or a restroom visit.

Because of its content, the suggested minimum age for the tour is 13 years old.

Photo credits: © Amsterdams Stadsarchief

name: Private: World War II and the Holocaust in Amsterdam
description:
Set out on a focused, three-hour private history walk that examines Amsterdam under Nazi occupation (1940–45). Led by retired historians, the tour visits important sites and monuments connected to this traumatic period, offering informed context and storytelling for visitors who want to go beyond typical tourist routes. The walk balances somber reflection with historical detail and includes a short break midway for coffee or a restroom visit. It is particularly suited to guests with a strong interest in twentieth-century history.

What's included

Private guided walk led by retired historians

Visits to important sites and monuments relating to the Nazi occupation

Short break midway for coffee and/or restroom visit

What's not included

Tickets for the Anne Frank House

Tickets for the Resistance Museum

Tickets for the Hollandsche Schouwburg

Drinks

Expert commentary from retired historians who specialise in the German occupation
Off-the-beaten-path sites that reveal lesser-known aspects of Amsterdam’s wartime history

name: Day Trip to Brugge
description:
Duration

12 Hours

## About

On this tour we will visit Bruges, one of the most beautiful cities of the Flemish region of Belgium. After a journey of approximately 3 hours, you will be able to enter on this magical city of Europe, walking through its historic centre, declared a World Heritage Site by the UNESCO. For 1 hour, one of our expert guides will introduce you to the most relevant aspects of Bruges by showing you its splendid churches, as well as its beautiful canals and alleyways and immersing you in the medieval atmosphere of a city that seems to have stopped in time.

Afterwards, you will have free time to taste the delicious Belgian mussels or their exclusive chocolates, refresh yourself with some of their famous beers, go shopping or discover another perspective of the city with a boat ride through its canals, among other options offered by a city.

Meeting Point (you will receive the address on Google Maps in your confirmation email)

De Ruijterkade 153, 1011AC Amsterdam (at the main door of the Powerzone bowling alley)

name: Authentic Amsterdam Private Tour: Jordaan - Anne Frank and Vondelpark
description:
Duration

3 hours

About

With this private tour we will get to know the most charming and emblematic areas of Amsterdam. With one of our expert guides we will talk about the past and present of the city, trying to get the most complete possible view of what is one of the most beautiful capitals in Europe.

Starting our tour at the Central Railway Station, we will enter the Ring of Canals, built in the 17th century and declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO. Here we will learn about the real palaces that were built by the wealthiest merchants during the Dutch golden age. Along the way, we will see the narrowest house in Amsterdam, as well as many of the famous boat houses and houseboats, in which the inhabitants of the city still live today.

Then we will discover what for many is the most beautiful district of Amsterdam, the Jordaan, known for its bohemian atmosphere and for being the cradle of great Dutch artists. In it, we will find the famous brown cafes, symbols of Amsterdammer life and Dutch culture, and we will w

name: Private Day Trip to the Windmills Zaanse Schans and the Enclosing Dike
description:
Duration

5 hours

About

Learn with an expert guide two of the greatest achievements in the history of the Netherlands: the Zaanse Schans mills and the Enclosing Dike "Afsluitdijk".

We will begin by heading to the Zaanse Schans Windmill Park, the economic engine of the country between the 16th and 18th centuries, as the Dutch took advantage of the force of the wind to grind the spices they brought from the colonies, make oils, mustards, etc. We will visit the amazing windmills and we will enjoy a workshop of clogs, where they will make a demonstration of the traditional Dutch footwear.

Then, we will head for the Enclosing Dike, which connects the provinces of North Holland and Friesland and which closed the South Sea forever, making it one of the largest lakes in Europe. It is the most important dam in the country, over 30 kilometres long, which slows the tides in the North Sea to avoid flooding.

As it is a private visit, the client can enjoy free time to take photos or make purchases. The itinerary can be agreed at the beginning of the tour to adapt it to the wishes of the client, alw

name: Private Day Trip the Keukenhof Gardens
description:
Duration

5 hours

About

Discover on this exclusive tour the Keukenhof Gardens, located in the west of the Netherlands, just 40 minutes from Amsterdam. It includes private vehicle, park entrance and a Spanish-speaking guide-driver.
Keukenhof has an extension of more than 32 hectares, being the largest floral exhibition in the world. Once in Keukenhof, one will be able to delight in impressive landscapes adorned with the most representative flower of the Dutch, the tulip, in addition to other varieties such as hyacinths, orchids and daffodils, among others.

During the journey from Amsterdam, an expert guide will carry out a historical contextualization to understand the close relationship between the tulip and the Netherlands, while passing through unique landscapes of the Dutch geography. Once in the park, there will be a guided tour of approximately 45 minutes. At the end of the explanations, you will enjoy about 3 hours of free time before returning to Amsterdam.

## We pick you up at your Hotel (City of Amsterdam)

Cancelaciones

name: Private Day Trip to Giethoorn
description:
Duration

9 hours

About

Did you know that Giethoorn has no roads? Its inhabitants travel by boat through the canals, so this Dutch town is also known as the "Green Venice". Leaving from Amsterdam, we will cross the province of Flevoland and the Weerriben - Wieden National Park until we reach Giethoorn.

There we will enjoy a 1-hour boat ride to contemplate the beauty and magic of the place, the old thatched farms and wooden bridges. We will also have free time to take pictures, stroll quietly and fill your stomach in one of its excellent restaurants (lunch is not included).

On the way to Enkhuizen we will stop at the port of Lelystad to see the replica of the "Batavia" (17th century galleon of the Dutch East India Company) and the famous statue "Exposure" by Antony Gormley.

We will cross from Flevoland to North Holland through the "Houtribdijk", a dam that connects both provinces and divides the Ijsselmeer and Markermeer lakes. Finally, we will visit Enkhuizen, an important city in the Dutch Golden Age and where time seems to have stopped. Its canals, bridges, historic buildings and po

name: Moco Boat Tour
description:
Come with us on one of our legendary canal cruises and get ready to cruise through Amsterdam's canals on a 100% electric boat.

Enjoy the view of Amsterdam from the water and cruise along past the city's famous landmarks.

Duration

1 hour

About

We embark in front of the Rijksmuseum. From there, we cruise through the oldest part of the city and you will discover the city's highlights and the beauty of the canals. In our one-hour canal cruise, we sail through Amsterdam's Golden Age canals, listed on the Unesco World Heritage List. We cruise into the Jordaan, a neighbourhood known for the Prinsengracht towards the romantic Skinny Bridge (English: Skinny Bridge - we will work on your Dutch pronunciation later), the Amstel River, the seven bridges, the impressive Golden Bend, and through the nine little streets back to the Rijksmuseum.

This canal cruise is sure to be the highlight of your visit to the city, so if you're looking for unique activities, you've come to the right place! With Flagship, you get to see the highlights of Amsterdam from a different perspective. Our local host will tell you all the secrets of the city and leave no question unanswered.

Tour is in Engl
highlights: Canal Cruise
Live guiding by the local skipper and hostess

name: Private Brewery Tour
description:
"The Amsterdam Brewery Tour kicks of in a small local brewery to give you a unique Dutch perspective on beer flavours and production! Next there’s an old Heineken brewery, renovated to provide an amazing tour experience. So why wait? Join the Amsterdam Brewery Tour!"

Includes:
Guide for 4 hours
Heineken Experience, incl. 2 beers Second Dutch Local Brewery Tour Tasting platter (4 beers)

name: [TNT] Open Boat Canal Cruise starts from Anne Frank
description:
Experience the beauty of Amsterdam from the water with KINboat's canal cruise. Sail along the historic canals, passing iconic landmarks like the Anne Frank House, Westerkerk, and the picturesque Magere Brug. Join us for a one-hour journey through the heart of the city's rich history and fashinating scenery.

Duration

60 minutes

What you see

Westerkerk, De Negen Straatjes, Prinsengracht, Royal Theater Carré, Magere Brug, H’ART Museum, Stopera, Groenburgwal, Dancing Houses Amsterdam, Herengracht, Hotel Seven Bridges, Het Grachtenhuis, Leidsche gracht.

About

Step aboard and embark on a mesmerizing journey through Amsterdam's enchanting waterways with KINboat's signature canal cruise. Our one-hour tour offers a perfect blend of history, culture, and panoramic views that will leave you mesmerized.

As we set sail from the Anne Frank House, you'll be greeted by the timeless charm of Amsterdam's iconic landmarks. Admire the towering spire of the Westerkerk, where Anne Frank once sought solace in its bells, and meander through the quaint alleys of the Negen Straatjes, known for their boutique shops and cozy cafes.

Duração

2h30min

Acerca

Tens curiosidade em conhecer um bairro típico holandês?

Então o De Pijp é o bairro que não podes perder! Irás conhecer um bairro construído no século XIX. No De Pijp, inicialmente, vivia a classe operária mas muito rapidamente se transformou no novo bairro onde viviam estudantes, artistas e prostitutas. Estamos a falar que este bairro passa a ser o Quartier Latin de Amesterdão, equiparando à zona académica do Quartier Latin perto de Paris. Actualmente é um dos bairros mais caros para viver em Amesterdão.

Vais ter a oportunidade de passear pelo parque Sarphatipark, ver o Mercado Albert Cuyp considerado o maior da Europa mas mais importante que isso vais ver a realidade do dia-a-dia holandês e a história linda desse bairro! Queremos que te percas connosco a contemplar a beleza do De Pijp.

Irás ter uma experiência única e pensada em ti uma vez que o tour é privado e tem em atenção o que pretendes levar da cidade. Se vens com crianças ou pessoas com mobilidade reduzida, não te preocupes que vos iremos ajudar e teremos em atenção o vosso ritmo.

O que vais conhecer no Tour

About

For those who really want to know Amsterdam there is only one thing you can do. That is of course to go with Captain Jack Amsterdam and his friends like many did before. On an amazing open boat with drinks and typical Dutch bytes we will introduce you to the amazing city. Day or night both times Amsterdam is amazing to see. And because we have all a very big connection and love for our city. We love to show you around and tell you the most amazing stories. Challenge us for our own personal stories to tell you and we can get completely ahead of ourselves.

We will take you to the best highlights of the city that can be seen from the water. Go from 2025 to 1275 in a heartbeat. Yes that is how old Amsterdam is. See the wealthy canal belt of th 16th century and have a bit of love with the skin

Join us for a delightful 45-minute adventure in Amsterdam, where you'll get the chance to make your very own stroopwafel, a classic Dutch delight. Our workshop is all about hands-on fun and discovering the joys of stroopwafel making.

45 minutes

We'll guide you through each step of crafting a stroopwafel, ensuring a fun and easy experience.

name: Open Boat Morning Cruise starts from Central Station
description:
Experience Amsterdam in its serene morning glory with KINboat's Morning Cruise. Set sail on the tranquil canals and immerse yourself in the peaceful ambiance of the city awakening. Perfect for those seeking a calm and leisurely start to their day, our morning cruise offers a unique perspective of Amsterdam as a quiet and charming city.

Duration

60 minutes

What you see

Red Light District, Central Station, Theater Carrè, Stopera former City Hall, Hortus Botanicus, Westerpark, National Maritime Museum

About

Wake up to the tranquil beauty of Amsterdam with KINboat's Morning Cruise, departing from the iconic Central Station. As the city slowly stirs to life, embark on a peaceful journey through Amsterdam's serene waterways, offering a unique perspective of the city as it awakens to a new day.

Setting sail from Central Station, you'll witness the quiet majesty of Amsterdam's waterfront in the soft morning light. Admire the grandeur of Central Station itself, a testament to the city's rich architectural heritage, before we navigate towards the National Maritime Museum and the former city hall Stopera.

As we sail along the tranquil canal

name: Cheese & Cheers Rondvaart Amsterdam
description:
De geur van vers gesneden kaas, een goed glas wijn in je hand en het zachte kabbelen van het water tegen de boot. Terwijl de luxe boot rustig door de Amsterdamse grachten glijdt, ontvouwt de stad zich voor je ogen. Historische gevels weerspiegelen in het water, fietsers schieten voorbij over eeuwenoude bruggen, terwijl de gids een anekdote vertelt over een koopman die ooit in een van de statige panden woonde. De Cheese & Cheers tour is niet zomaar een rondvaart. Dit is een ontspannen ontdekkingsreis langs de mooiste plekken van Amsterdam, met een goed gevulde borrelplank en een verhaal bij iedere bocht in de gracht.

name: Rijksmuseum Guided Museum Tour - Private (SPANISH)
description:
Holland’s version of the Louvre is essential when visiting Amsterdam, and this private tour will introduce visitors to the seemingly endless collection that curates Dutch history over the centuries. This 2 to 2.5 hour private guided tour of the Rijksmuseum will pair you with a dashing young guide who will lead you through the museum. Your guide will provide tickets that are valid the whole day so that you can return at your leisure.

For those who aren’t familiar with Dutch culture – and who is really? – we will explore its rich and vibrant past through a selection of works from among the 8000 objects on display. We’ll discuss familiar names like Rembrandt as well as lesser known items like 17th century dollhouses. Understand the meaning behind Vermeer’s “The Milkmaid” and marvel at the expansive 19th century library. At the end of the tour, you’ll have a good overview of the museum’s collection and you may even know how to pronounce its name correctly.

Cancellations
highlights: Youth - €79 (Ages 10 - 17)
Child - €59 (9 and under)
2 - 2.5 hours
Meet your guide outside the Cobra Cafe by the Toilet/Parking entrance on the north side of the building facing the Rijksmuseum. (between the bicycle lane and the Cobra Cafe)

name: Rembrandt’s City & Home Guided Tour - Private (SPANISH)
description:
Retrace Rembrandt’s steps as you explore the district where the artist once lived. On this 2.5-hour private guided tour, your guide will lead you to explore Amsterdam’s streets, visiting the sights and places that inspired some of Rembrandt’s iconic paintings, before having a guided visit of his house with skip the line access.

Begin by following your guide through the Spiegelquarter, the traditional district for all things art and antiques. You’ll then walk onwards towards the Keizersgracht, or Emperor’s canal, to learn about one of Rembrandt’s best-known apprentices. In Rembrandt Square, discover the Night Watch Guards – one of the artist’s most famous works – which is recreated in bronze sculpture. Further on in the Staalstraat, hear about the local Drapers Guild, immortalized by Rembrandt, before learning about the old Jewish quarter around Waterloo Square. Rembrandt and his wife Saskia lived here, near the Amstel River, and you will visit the artist’s house, now a museum celebrating his contributions to Dutch art. The museum features the original house with a meticulous collection of 17th century period pieces, reconstructed thanks to historical auction records. Wander the etchings, artefacts, and rooms as Rembrandt himself once did. His small office and other personal items offer a glimpse into the life of this artistic genius.

Cancellations
highlights: Youth - €79 (Ages 10 - 17)
Child - €59 (9 and under)
Meet your guide outside the Cobra Cafe by the Toilet/Parking entrance on the north side of the building facing the Rijksmuseum. (between the bicycle lane and the Cobra Cafe)
You’re curious about Rembrandt, we get it. There’s no better reason to join a private tour in Amsterdam
On this 2.5-hour private tour, you will walk through the streets and square that inspired the 17th century artist’s works, ending with a guided visit of his house.
This 2.5 hour semi-private walking tour is the perfect introduction to the life of Rembrandt.
This tour is in English.

This 2.5 hour semi-private walking tour is the perfect introduction to the life of Rembrandt.
This tour is in English.

name: Rembrandt’s City & Home Guided Tour - Private (ITALIAN)
description:
Retrace Rembrandt’s steps as you explore the district where the artist once lived. On this 2.5-hour private guided tour, your guide will lead you to explore Amsterdam’s streets, visiting the sights and places that inspired some of Rembrandt’s iconic paintings, before having a guided visit of his house with skip the line access.

Begin by following your guide through the Spiegelquarter, the traditional district for all things art and antiques. You’ll then walk onwards towards the Keizersgracht, or Emperor’s canal, to learn about one of Rembrandt’s best-known apprentices. In Rembrandt Square, discover the Night Watch Guards – one of the artist’s most famous works – which is recreated in bronze sculpture. Further on in the Staalstraat, hear about the local Drapers Guild, immortalized by Rembrandt, before learning about the old Jewish quarter around Waterloo Square. Rembrandt and his wife Saskia lived here, near the Amstel River, and you will visit the artist’s house, now a museum celebrating his contributions to Dutch art. The museum features the original house with a meticulous collection of 17th century period pieces, reconstructed thanks to historical auction records. Wander the etchings, artefacts, and rooms as Rembrandt himself once did. His small office and other personal items offer a glimpse into the life of this artistic genius.

Cancellations
highlights: Youth - €79 (Ages 10 - 17)
Child - €59 (9 and under)
Meet your guide outside the Cobra Cafe by the Toilet/Parking entrance on the north side of the building facing the Rijksmuseum. (between the bicycle lane and the Cobra Cafe)

name: Combo: Rijksmuseum + Rembrandt’s City & Home Skip-the-Line Guided Combo Tour - Private (SPANISH)
description:
No trip to Amsterdam is complete without an introduction to its artistic institutions. This private guided combo tour ticks all the boxes with a visit to the Rijksmuseum, followed by a tour focusing on the life of world-famous artist Rembrandt. Relax and enjoy during this private 5 to 5.5 hour tour, which includes skip the line entry so you can maximize every moment.

Your tour starts at Holland’s answer to the Louvre, the Rijksmuseum, housing centuries of Dutch history and art. Explore over 8000 objects, including paintings and artefacts, with artists from the Dutch Golden Age like Rembrandt and Vermeer on display. From dollhouses to a captured British warship, the museum boasts a wide range of historical items. The largest museum in Amsterdam, a visit is an absolute must.

After lunch, dig deeper into Rembrandt’s life to learn all about this iconic Dutch artist. Walk through Spiegelquarter, a district known for a wide array of antiques and artistic goods. Afterwards, wander the Keizersgracht, or Emperor’s canal, where you’ll discuss one of Rembrandt’s most successful apprentices. In Rembrandt Square you’ll hear about the Night Watch Guards, who were characters in one of the artist’s most popular works. His painting is recreated here in bronze sculpture. In Staalstraat, learn about the local Drapers Guild, represented by Rembrandt. Finally, in Waterloo Square, you will discover the former Jewish Quarter, home to a variety of immigrants including Rembrandt and his wife. The couple spent many years living here, near the Amstel River, where their house still stands as a museum dedicated to the artist’s life. It includes etchings and other works done by Rembrandt himself. The furnishings are authentic to the 1600s, reconstituted thanks to auction archives from the period. Wander Rembrandt’s office and see how he produced his work, peeking at the more intimate side of the artist’s life.

Cancellations
highlights: Youth - €139 (Ages 10 - 17)
Child - €105 (9 and under)
5 - 5.5 hours (including a lunch break)
Meet your guide outside the Cobra Cafe by the Toilet/Parking entrance on the north side of the building facing the Rijksmuseum. (between the bicycle lane and the Cobra Cafe)
For those who want to be familiar with Rembrandt's life and works – we will explore his rich and vibrant past on this popular combo tour.
The 5 to 5.5 hour semi-private tour will include a visit to the Rijksmuseum followed by a Rembrandt themed walking tour and guided visit through his house.
Your guide will provide you with a ticket that allows you to skip the line at the Rijksmuseum.

name: Excursión Privada Utrecht
description:
Duración

5 Horas

Acerca

En esta excursión a Utrecht, saldremos de Amsterdam en nuestro transporte privado para descubrir una de las ciudades con mayor encanto de Holanda de la mano de uno de nuestros guías hispanohablantes.

En Utrecht recorreremos sus canales Oudegracht y Nieuwegracht, famosos por su forma única en el mundo, con muelles, bodegas, bares y restaurantes a la altura del agua

Por sus calles laberínticas descubriremos las iglesias románicas en forma de cruz sobre el mapa de la ciudad, siendo la primera vez en la historia del cristianismo que se realiza el plano de la ciudad en función a la situación de las iglesias.

Admiraremos la famosa Torre del Dom, que es el punto más alto de la ciudad y el monumento histórico más elevado de todo el país y otros edificios históricos como la Academiegebouw, el Pandhof o el lugar donde se firmó el Tratado de Utrecht.

Al tratarse de una visita privada, el cliente podrá disfrutar de tiempo libre para hacer fotos o realizar compras. El itinerario podrá pactarse al comienzo del tour para adaptarlo a los deseos del cliente, siempre considerand

name: Excursión Privada Utrecht y Castillo de Haar
description:
Duración

7 Horas

Acerca

Combina con esta excursión privada una de las ciudades con mayor encanto de Holanda y la visita al castillo más grande de todo el país, de la mano de uno de nuestros guías hispanohablantes.

En Utrecht recorreremos sus canales Oudegracht y Nieuwegracht, famosos por su forma única en el mundo, con muelles, bodegas, bares y restaurantes a la altura del agua.

Por sus calles laberínticas descubriremos las iglesias románicas en forma de cruz sobre el mapa de la ciudad, siendo la primera vez en la historia del cristianismo que se realiza el plano de la ciudad en función a la situación de las iglesias.

Admiraremos la famosa Torre del Dom, que es el punto más alto de la ciudad y el monumento histórico más elevado de todo el país y otros edificios históricos como la Academiegebouw, el Pandhof o el lugar donde se firmó el Tratado de Utrecht.

A continuación, visitaremos el Castillo de Haar, de aspecto medieval, aunque en realidad se construyó a principios del siglo XX y fue diseñado por el famoso arquitecto Pierre Cuypers, conocido por diseñar otros edificios emblemáticos

name: Ticket Moco Museum
description:
Horario de Apertura del Museo

De 9.00 h a 19.00 h

Acerca

El Museo Moco de Amsterdam es un museo boutique con una amplia gama de inspiradores arte moderno y contemporáneo. Con artistas como Banksy, Andy Warhol y Roy Lichtenstein, ofrecemos a los visitantes una colección incomparable de arte subversivo en el que la ironía y el humor se utilizan para reflexionar sobre la sociedad moderna. Una experiencia única dirigida a un público amplio, una visita a Moco es una verdadera revelación.

Cancelaciones

name: Private Red Tour
description:
Duration

2 hours

About

Through a private walking tour we will discover the oldest area of the city with one of our professional guides resident in Amsterdam.

We will enter this area of narrow streets full of secrets. The guide will tell us the culture and regulation of drugs through coffeshops and smartshops, as well as a review of the history of prostitution to the present day in the city of Amsterdam.

As this is a more exclusive and private visit, the client will be able to adapt the itinerary according to his preferences.

We’ll pick you up at your hotel in downtown Amsterdam. In case your hotel is outside the city centre, the meeting point will be at I Amsterdam Visitor Centre (Stationsplein 10, opposite the Central Train Station)
Cancelaciones

name: Amsterdam Private Panoramic Tour
description:
Duration

3 hours

About

With this private tour we will get to know in depth the most charming and emblematic areas of Amsterdam. From the hand of one of our expert guides we will talk about the past and present of the city, trying to obtain the most complete vision possible of what is one of the most beautiful capitals in Europe.

Starting our tour at the Central Train Station, we will enter the Ring of Canals, built in the seventeenth century and declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO. Here we will learn about the authentic palaces that were built by the wealthiest merchants during the Dutch Golden Age. Along the way, we will see the narrowest house in Amsterdam, as well as many of the famous boat houses and houseboats, in which the inhabitants of the city still live today.

Next, we will discover what for many is the most beautiful neighborhood in Amsterdam, the Jordaan, known for its bohemian atmosphere and for being the birthplace of great Dutch artists. In it, we will find the famous brown cafes, symbols of Amsterdammer life and Dutch culture, and we will walk through the s

name: Amsterdam Private Panoramic Tour and Zaanse Schans Windmills
description:
Duration

5 hours

About

With this pack combines in the same day our two tours: The panoramic tour of Amsterdam with a visit to the windmill park of Zaanse Schans.

Once you have visited the city by private car, visiting the most representative areas of Amsterdam, you will head to the Zaanse Schans Windmill Park, the economic motor of the country between the 16th and 18th centuries, as the Dutch took advantage of the force of the wind to grind the spices they brought from the colonies, to make oils, mustards, etc. We will enter a mill dedicated to spices and a workshop of clogs, where they will give us a demonstration of traditional Dutch shoe making.

As it is a private visit, the client can enjoy free time to take photos or make purchases. The itinerary can be agreed at the beginning of the tour to adapt it to the wishes of the client, always considering the duration of the tour.

We will pick you up at your Hotel (City of Amsterdam)
Cancelaciones

name: Private Day Trip to Utrecht and Haar Castle
description:
Duration

7 hours

About

This tour combines a private excursion to one of the most charming cities in the Netherlands, Utrecht, with a visit to the largest castle in the whole country. And of course, you will be accompanied by one of our professional guides!

In Utrecht we will visit its canals Oudegracht and Nieuwegracht, famous for their unique shape in the world, with quays, wineries, bars and restaurants at the height of the water.

Through its labyrinthine streets we will discover the Romanesque cross-shaped churches on the city map, being the first time in the history of Christianity that the city plan is realized according to the situation of the churches.

We will admire the famous Tower of the Dom, which is the highest point of the city and the highest historical monument in the whole country and other historic buildings such as the Academiegebouw, the Pandhof or the place where the Treaty of Utrecht was signed.

Then we will visit the Castle of Haar, with a medieval air, but in fact it was built at the beginning of the twentieth century and was designed by the famous architect P

name: Private Day Trip to Rotterdam - The Hague and Scheveningen
description:
Duration

9 hours

About

In this private excursion we will start heading to the city of Rotterdam, where we will make a panoramic visit, also making some stop to observe from close up places like the Markthal, the Church of Saint Laurent or the famous Cube Houses.

Rotterdam had to be completely rebuilt after the bombing suffered in the Second World War, therefore, we will find a city different from Amsterdam, highlighting its contemporary architecture innovative and groundbreaking, referring to world-wide.

Next, we will go to The Hague, the administrative capital of the country, where the Parliament is located, a group of buildings that is known as "Binnenhof", being the oldest parliamentary complex in use in the world. Large avenues, majestic buildings, palaces, embassies and large expanses of green areas give this city a stately air.

On our tour we will also visit the "Palais Noordeinde", the King’s work center, the prestigious museum of Mauritshuis and the Peace Palace, seat of the International Court of Justice, where we will make a stop.

To finish the tour we will head for Scheve

name: Private Day Trip to Bruges
description:
Duration

12 Hours

About

On this private trip departing from Amsterdam you will visit Bruges, one of the most beautiful cities of the Flemish region of Belgium. After a journey of about 3 hours, it will be possible to discover this magical city in Europe, walking through its historic centre, declared a World Heritage Site by the UNESCO. For just over an hour, one of our expert guides will introduce the most relevant aspects of Bruges by showing its splendid churches, as well as its beautiful canals and alleys and diving into the medieval atmosphere of a city that seems to have stopped in time.

Afterwards, you will have more than 3 hours of free time to taste the delicious Belgian mussels or their exclusive chocolates, refresh yourself with some of their famous beers, go shopping or discover another perspective of the city with a boat ride through its canals, among other options offered by a city that will certainly not leave anyone indifferent.

As a private tour, the itinerary can be arranged at the beginning of the day to adapt it to the wishes of the client, always considering t

name: Amsterdam Authentic Tour: Jordaan, Anne Frank and Leidseplein
description:
Duration

3 hours

About

As if we were in a time machine, the authentic Amsterdam tour will transport you back in time to learn in detail how the Dutch lived centuries ago. But of course! We cannot forget about the present, and for that reason, we will see together what is happening in this city today. A 360 view of all what has happened in this emblematic city.

Cancelaciones

name: Private Open Boat Sundeck Cruise
description:
Discover the old Amsterdam canals from a typical local boat, and see the city and it's architecture in a modern open sundeck boat.

The 90 minute cruise will mainly show the canal belt scenery, Amstel river, the east side of the centre and more.

The boat can fit low bridges narrow passages, allowing to find more secluded canals and hidden gems of the city.
The skippers are local and operating in a personal setting. With their extensive knowledge of the city and experience as a skipper, they are always interested to engage with the guests and elaborate on any questions you might have about the city.

What's included

A full 360 perspective of Amsterdam scenery

What's not included

Add drinks during your booking and enjoy!

Additional information
Cancellations
highlights: A full 360 perspective of Amsterdam scenery

name: Shared Beer & Bubbly Bike
description:
Duration

75 minutes

About

Jump on board our Amsterdam Beer Bike for the only tour in town that promises to keep you refreshed every step of the way!

Amsterdam Beer Bikes, you and your party will enjoy drinks on the streets of Amsterdam in the area of Sloterdijk, a thriving new municipality of the city !

Each Beer Bike is equipped with a beer tap as well as extra beer cans for backup. The Amsterdam Beer Bike is a fantastic activity to celebrate special occasions such as bachelor/bachelorette parties, or corporate outings.

Included
Beer or Prosecco can be chosen
Cancellations
highlights: Beer or Prosecco can be chosen

description:
Duration

75 minutes

About

Jump on board our Amsterdam Beer Bike for the only tour in town that promises to keep you refreshed every step of the way!

On board our licensed Amsterdam Beer Bikes, you and your party will enjoy drinks on the streets of Amsterdam in the area of Sloterdijk, a thriving new municipality of the city!

Have a blast pedalling through the city and making memories with your friends.

name: Highland Games
description:

2 hours

name: Public: World War II Cycle Tour
description:
€55 p.p | 3 hrs | Bicycles are included

	1198 Reviewers
#43 of 1,340 Tours in Amsterdam
TripAdvisor Traveler Rating
According to TripAdvisor travelers as of April 2026
Public tour: * World War II Cycle Tour*
Rates

Adult (13+): €55

Duration

3 hours

Bicycles are included
About

The starting point of the tour is the bicycle rental 'Instabikel', Tesselschadestraat 1, close to Leidseplein, where you will receive your bicycle.

Amsterdam South has many monuments and buildings from World War II, including the house where Anne Frank lived with her parents and sister before they went into hiding.

Places where the round up (infamous razzia) of the Jewish citizens living in this part of the city took place.

The German army headquarters and civil services, the headquarters of the Dutch Nazi party (NSB-National Socialist Movement) in Amsterdam all concentrated at Museumplein. In 1941 Museumplein was visited by SS and Gestapo (the German secret police) chief Heinrich Himmler.

The Gestapo headquarters Amsterdam, which was partially destroyed by allied ait attack in 1944. Resistance safe houses.

We also visit places where executions, raids and battles between the Dutch resistance and German soldiers took place.

The tours are given by three retired historians with a passion for the history of the German occupation during the Second World War.

The bike tour will not visit places from the WW2 walking tour.

name: Private Group Van Gogh Museum Tour
description:
Discover the largest collection of Vincent Van Gogh’s work in the world, in this tour of the Van Gogh museum. The museum is home to many of his paintings, including The Potato Eaters, one of the Sunflowers paintings, the Bedroom, and an assortment of landscapes and nature scenes. You don’t have to be an expert to enjoy art, as our knowledgeable guide will make it accessible and interesting to your students.

GET UP CLOSE TO VAN GOGH'S WORK

On this tour, your students will discover one of the most famous and influential painters in the history of art, Vincent Van Gogh. Known as a misunderstood genius, he was considered a madman and a failure while he was alive. He quickly acquired widespread artistic and commercial success in the decades following his death.

EXPLORE THE LIFE OF AN ARTIST

More than just a tour of his masterpieces, in this tour your group will learn about Van Gogh as a person. How did he start his painting career? How was his relationship with his family? You'll follow Vincent’s story from his humble upbringing, his decision to take up painting, to his most productive times and his eventual tragic death. Why is his art so important? How did he develop his famously bold colours and impulsive brushwork? How did Van Gogh become one of the most celebrated painters of post- impressioni

name: Private Group Rijksmuseum Tour
description:
Discover the greatest collection of Dutch art in a fun and exciting way with our friendly guide. You don’t have to be an expert to enjoy art, as our knowledgeable guide will make it accessible and interesting to your students.

GET UP CLOSE TO DUTCH MASTERPIECES

See the masterpieces of Rembrandt, Vermeer, and Jan Steen with easily understood explanations that highlight the historic value and meaning of the artworks. Your group will leave with an understanding of what makes the Night Watch so significant and how a hobby painter like Vermeer left such a lasting impression.

VISIT THE GALLERY OF HONOUR

Tours of the Rijksmuseum vary depending on the interests and learning goals of the group, so please inform us of any specific curriculum targets or areas of study. We aim to include the Gallery of Honour, as that is very much the 'greatest hits' of the collection, and several other areas of the museum, including the beautiful library, You’ll hear quirky stories about the artists, fun facts about their work, and how their work contributed to the Dutch cultural heritage.

EXPLORE THE 'CATHEDRAL OF ART'

It's not only the paintings that will be discussed but also the impressive, recently renovated building. After more than a decade of renovation, the Rijksmuseum reopened in 2013 - a work of art

name: Anne Frank's life in Amsterdam
description:
We will walk in the footsteps of Anne Frank and learn about the World War 2 stories, events and motives from a Jewish, Dutch and German perspective.

I will take you to the places Anne Frank lived, played and went to school before going into hiding, places that look today almost exactly like it was in her time.

And by following her footsteps, we will learn about many more people, places and events to tell the story of the German occupation of the Netherlands during World War 2.

By visiting the sites and telling the stories of normal people under extreme circumstances we will bring the time of the war closer to home. With the use of authentic photos and videos, and by explaining the events from all perspectives, we will find out about the motives of the people involved, and we will ultimately ask the question:

Could this happen again in our time?

Meeting point at Amsterdam Central Station, or pick-up available within Amsterdam city only.
Walking tour in Anne Frank's Amsterdam
What's not included

name: Public Cheese Walk & Tasting Workshop
description:
This is NOT just a cheese tasting - it is an entertaining and educational experience!

We will take you to some of the best cheese shops in Europe. There, we will buy four or five very special cheeses for you to enjoy. The cheese selection will vary, depending on the season and what I think is special that day as well as what will suit you. The cheeses are very different to what tourists normally try in Amsterdam!

We will then walk to a 300 year old Tavern to enjoy the cheeses with 4 very special beers, that pair well with the cheese (or 2 glasses of wine). We can also visit a wine shop instead.
There you will learn how the different cheeses are made, also, what makes them look, smell and taste different. You will also learn how to choose, travel with and store cheese. Even how best to cut and serve cheese!

Met jarenlange ervaring in het organiseren van allerlei gelegenheden hoef jij alleen nog maar te ontspannen en te genieten.

Huur een van onze prachtige open boten of klassieke salonboten, altijd inclusief professionele schipper. Perfect voor een verjaardag, babyshower, vrijgezellenfeest (sorry, geen strippers 😉), een huwelijksaanzoek of een zakelijk event.

About

The name says it all, come on board with your friends or colleagues and get unlimited beer, wine and soda for everyone while you explore the canals of Amsterdam on one of our luxurious open boats.

Have you ever wondered what it would be like to cruise through the canals like a king, while having drinks with your friends while no one has to worry about the tap that has to be payed or random people joining your party? Then this is the best plan for you!

Our local skipper will be able to answer any of your questions while you explore all the secrets the canals of Amsterdam. With a choice between wine, beers or all sorts of sodas (or all three of them) we have something for everyone and no one gets left out.

name: Privé E-biketour door Amsterdam
description:
Met een enthousiaste gids ontdekt u de verhalen bij bekende bezienswaardigheden van Amsterdam. Daarnaast fietsen we langs prachtige onbekende plekjes. Zo bezoekt u tijdens de grote tour de oude Westelijke Eilanden, een van de mooiste buurten in het centrum van Amsterdam, grotendeels vrij van bezoekers.

U zit natuurlijk niet de hele tijd op de fiets. De gids stopt regelmatig voor een verhaal en halverwege de fietstour pauzeren we om iets te drinken. Deze 3 uur tour is minder intensief dan de 2 uur tour: doordat de tour een pauze bevat, is het een rustig uitstapje en zeer geschikt om te rijden met zowel vrienden, familie als collega’s.

De tour heeft een rustig tempo, toch zie je veel buurten van Amsterdam.

Vriendelijke lokale gids
E-bike (Minimumlengte: 152 cm of 5ft)
Regenponcho
25% korting op fietshuur na de tour
20 min pauze in het Vondelpark

name: The Amsterdam Breakfast Tour with Stroopwafel Making
description:
Embark on the The Amsterdam Breakfast Tour with Stroopwafel Making, a delicious journey through the city's traditional Dutch delights. Explore the history of family-run shops and cafes. This breakfast tour offers an intimate glimpse into Amsterdam’s iconic Albert Cuyp Market in de Pijp neighbourhood. A vibrant marketplace that has been at the heart of the city for over 120 years.

At the end of our tour, our guides can point you in the right direction or call a taxi for you (or give you a recommendation of what to see next nearby).

Prices may vary due to seasonality

What's included

Saucijzen
highlights: Saucijzenbroodjes (Dutch sausage roll)
Gouda cheese
Dutch-style ham
Savory pancakes
Stroopwafel

name: Amsterdam Light Festival Canal Cruise on Luxury Boat
description:
Sail with us through the canals of Amsterdam and admire the impressive light art of the 14th edition of the Amsterdam Light Festival. This year’s theme, Legacy, invites artists from around the world to reflect on what we preserve and what we pass on to future generations.

During this 75-minute cruise, you will encounter more than 20 light artworks that transform the city into an open-air art festival. Experience it from a comfortable, luxury open boat, offering panoramic views of the illuminated canals.

Drinks such as wine, mulled wine, beer, and soft drinks are available on board. If you choose the unlimited drinks option, you’ll enjoy all of these beverages without limits, along with your choice of a fresh stroopwafel or a portion of salted snacks to complete the experience.

Step aboard, take in the unique perspectives from the open water, and be part of an inspiring celebrat
highlights: 75-minute Cruise along the artworks of the Amsterdam Light Festival
Experienced local skipper and hosts
Warm Blankets
Beer, wine, mulled wine, and soft drinks (if option chosen)
Small bite or a stroopwafel (if option chosen)
75-min Canal Cruise during the 14th edition of the Amsterdam Light Festival
Enjoy the festival from a classic, luxurious open boat

name: Private Group Amsterdam’s Ghostly Experience
description:
GHOSTS OF AMSTERDAM

The dark history of almost 800 years hangs heavily over Amsterdam, and the past is lurking down every dark alley and street corner. Ancient souls wander Amsterdam’s many streets and buildings, endlessly searching, but for what? We do not know.

Bloody tales of murder, betrayal and even madness driven by love and jealousy flow through the many stories of troubled shadows that appear, mainly at night. Yet most locals will tell you they do not believe in ghosts…

EXPLORE DARK ALLEYS

With a lantern by their side, our guide will take the group on a quest to uncover the darker side of Amsterdam as we hear tales full of darkness and treachery. On this fun tour, our guide will share some of the history of Amsterdam, along with some of the spookier stories from the city. Whilst the focus of this tour is very much on fun, our guide will share some of the darker tales of the city, which can be adapted so that they are age-appropriate to the group.

SEE THE CITY AT NIGHT

The city looks magical at night, with the lights on the bridges and reflections in the canals, so this tour isn't all darkness. Although, who knows who or what we may discover as we explore the city in the darkest depths of night? On these tours, the spirits have a way of making their presence felt…

*This tour can be adapt
highlights: Unique route with stories through the city.
Personal guide for 2 hours

description:
Explore Amsterdam de uma Perspectiva Única!
Descubra a beleza e a história de Amsterdam a bordo de um passeio de barco pelos famosos canais, reconhecidos como Patrimônio Mundial da UNESCO. Navegue por entre as icônicas casas inclinadas, pontes encantadoras e monumentos históricos enquanto aprecia uma vista deslumbrante da cidade.

O que está incluído

O Que Esperar do Passeio de Barco por Amsterdam?
✔️ Passeio confortável e relaxante pelos canais icônicos de Amsterdam.
✔️ Audioguia em português para conhecer a história e curiosidades da cidade.
✔️ Vistas panorâmicas das principais atrações turísticas, incluindo a Casa de Anne Frank, a Magere Brug (Ponte Estreita) e o Bairro Jordaan.
✔️ Horários flexíveis, com saídas ao longo do dia.
✔️ Ideal para todas as idades – perfeito para casais, famílias e grupos de amigos.

name: BLACK FRIDAY: Open Boat Canal Cruise from Anne Frank House
description:
Beleef de schoonheid van Amsterdam vanaf het water met de grachtentour van KINboat. Vaar langs de historische grachten en ontdek iconische bezienswaardigheden zoals het Anne Frank Huis, de Westerkerk en de schilderachtige Magere Brug. Ga met ons mee op een één uur durende tocht door het hart van de stad, vol rijke geschiedenis en fascinerende uitzichten.

name: Sail Amsterdam
description:
Vier samen met ons het 750-jarig bestaan van Amsterdam tijdens de 10de editie van SAIL Amsterdam, een van ’s werelds grootste maritieme evenementen!

SAIL wordt slechts eens in de vijf jaar georganiseerd en brengt een indrukwekkende vloot samen van majestueuze Tall Ships, marineschepen, historische replica’s en moderne schepen van over de hele wereld.

En hoe beleef je dit spektakel het best? Vanaf het water, natuurlijk!
Als officiële partner van SAIL brengt Amsterdam Boat Trips je dichter bij de actie dan ooit, met een uniek uitzicht op deze indrukwekkende schepen vanaf je eigen plekje op het water.

Mis dit onvergetelijke avontuur niet en boek vandaag nog!

Wat is erbij inbegrepen

-Onbeperkt drankjes inbegrepen – bier, wijn, frisdrank
-Unieke 2 uur durende vaartocht midden in het spektakel van SAIL

Wat is niet inbegrepen

-Geen toilet aan boord
-Nier rolstoeltoegankelijk

Highlights

-Unieke kans – slechts eens in de 5 jaar!
-Vier 750 jaar Amsterdam tijdens het grootste maritieme evenement van Nederland
-Vaar tussen de wereldberoemde Tall Ships en historische schepen

Aanvullende informatie
Veelgestelde vragen
Annuleringen

name: Romantic Canal Cruise Experience in Amsterdam
description:
Create Your Perfect Evening

Let Amsterdam’s charming canals set the stage for a truly romantic experience. Reserve your romantic canal cruise today and enjoy the magic of the city with your special someone.

Our cozy boat provides the perfect ambiance for a romantic evening, complete with soft lighting and scenic views. Whether you’re celebrating an anniversary, a special occasion, or simply enjoying time together, this cruise offers a peaceful and magical escape in the heart of Amsterdam.

Discover romance on Amsterdam’s iconic canals with your loved one. Create unforgettable memories as you glide past the city’s historic bridges, charming houseboats, and illuminated waterways on an intimate journey tailored for couples.

What's included

• Bottle of wine or bubbles
• Luxurious cheese platter with fine selection of cheeses, fruits and chocolates

name: Anne Frank’s Amsterdam
description:
Anne Frank is Amsterdam’s most famous resident. Every year millions of people travel to Amsterdam to see where Anne and her family hid from the Nazis during WW2. In this tour, we will walk along the last part of the route Anne and her family took on their journey to their hiding place, and sadly their final home. Together, we will travel back in time to the Amsterdam of WWII and experience the emotion and hope of Anne Frank during the dark days of the war as her story unfolds.

As we walk around the local area we’ll see the famous Westerkerk, the outside of the house where Anne and her family were hiding for over 2 years and the beautiful Prinsengracht. Using Anne’s own words from her diary, we will learn about Anne, the ‘secret annex’, what she could see from her refuge and the influence her story has had on people in the Netherlands and the rest of the world. Rather than a sombre story of loss and sorrow, this tour is also about tolerance and hope, because, as Anne wrote: "Don’t think of all the misery, but of all the beauty that still remains."

Our tours in Amsterdam are small-scale and personal with a maximum of 15 people per guide.

What's included

Unique route with stories through

name: Knitting Lesson with Guidance in the Craft Café - Make your own Project
description:
Want to learn the basics of knitting or get some help on a project you're working on? Then join us for a lesson in our Craft Café!

Craft tables are available with tools and guidance by our craft experts, ready to help you with your projects.

You can bring your own ideas to the lessons (photos, drawings, or patterns of the pieces you want to create). Together we can see how we can realise your project. You can also take a look at our store which has some patterns and materials available. Send your project wishes and ideas to depijp@desteekamsterdam.nl before you come.

Important to Know

• Level: all levels are welcome (complete beginner to advanced)
• Language: Dutch & English
• If there is enough space, it’s always possible to extend your stay and pay extra for the hours in class
• Prices per person

Address: Van der Hoopstraat 70HS, 1051 VK, Amsterdam

What's included

• Guidance by craft experts to help you with your own projects.
• Use of tools are included in the lessons.
• Water and cookies are also at your disposal.

What's not included

• Materials you have to provide yourself are: yarns, stitch markers, and, when needed, supplies like a zippers or buttons.

name: Beginner Knitting Workshop: Chunky Scarf
description:
Have you always wanted to learn to knitting, but never got around to it? We have developed a fun course in which you can master the basics of knitting in 3 lessons; 2 lessons of 2 hours and 1 lesson of 1 hour, bookable in our Craft Café! Start at the beginning and knit your very first stitches. While learning the techniques, you will also make a chunky scarf. This course will teach you the basics and prepare you to work on your own projects at home. Choose to have the materials in- or excluded.

What you will learn:

How to start your work (cast on), knit & purl, garter stitch, stockinette stitch, and finishing.

Important to Know

You are paying for the complete workshop upon completing your booking.

• Tea

Tijdens de rondvaart bewonder je de schitterende lichtkunstwerken die de grachten van Amsterdam omtoveren tot een levend kunstwerk. Dit arrangement biedt de ideale combinatie van licht, sfeer en culinaire beleving - een complete avond uit die je niet wilt missen.

name: Company Beach Cleanup Rabobank - GoodUp
description:
Stop talking, let’s start doing! Together, we show our commitment. Beach cleanups are fun, and most people find it hard to stop once they begin. This is precisely our goal. Together, we can expand our community of proactive individuals and work towards a plastic free land & sea.

Participating in a beach cleanup provides the ideal ingredients for an unforgettable day. Come join us with your colleagues for an exciting outing that not only aligns with our mission but also blends environmental involvement with the joy of the winter season!

Duration

2 hours

About

We're on a mission to create a plastic free land & sea, and we want you to be a part of our mission! Through the power of teamwork, we're turning cleanup efforts into joyful experiences that leave us all with a fulfilling smile. The biggest winner in this mission is our precious planet, and by joining us, you're making a meaningful contribution to its preservation.

As we’re taking action together, you'll not only make a positive impact but also gain valuable knowledge about the global plastic problem. This journey will equip you and your colleagues with insights on how you can play a vital role in creating a cleaner and greener environment. So, let's start doing!

Program

10 min - Warm welcome with a drink
10 min - Introduction by Plastic Whale
10 mi
highlights: 20 – 150 people
Cleanup materials

IJmuiden/Zandvoort/Scheveningen/Hoek van Holland

description:
A full 360 perspective of Amsterdam scenery

Classic Amsterdam Launch boat with silent electric drive.

About

Discover the old Amsterdam canals and see the modern architecture in a classic, one-of-a-kind historical boat.
The 60 minute cruise is stylish, exlusive and informative with local skippers operating in a personal setting. With their extensive knowledge of the city and experience as a skipper, they are always interested to engage with the guests and elaborate on any questions you might have about the city.

The cruise is intimate and has no more than 12 guests on board. There is heating and blankets, and guests can choose to either sit inside or outside.
There are multiple options for drinks and snacks.

name: Old Harbour Tour
description:
Ever wondered how a swampy outpost became a global powerhouse? Our passionate Dutch historian guides will be your compass. They'll paint a vivid picture of medieval Amsterdam's meteoric rise to become a "pre-modern" global powerhouse within just a few decades. Discover the cascade of inventions and intrepid personalities that laid the foundation for this remarkable transformation. Learn how a humble fish, the herring, played a pivotal role in Amsterdam's success.

Everywhere you turn, Amsterdam's maritime heritage is evident. We'll navigate past the Open Harbor Museum and admire a replica of an 18th-century merchant ship at the National Maritime Museum. At the Marineterrein, witness how Amsterdam is reinventing itself, embracing new technologies to remain a relevant player on the global stage.

This captivating walk is for anyone who loves adventure, history, and wants to gain a deeper understanding of Amste
highlights: Private guide
Visit to a 100 years old shipping company building
Visit to the former Amsterdam Navy Dockyard

name: Red Tour Amsterdam
description:
Do you really want to know how the famous Red Light District works? Through this walking tour you will discover the oldest area of the city, where the Red Light District is currently located. In it, an expert guide will show the most hidden corners of this famous neighborhood and talk about the history, functioning and current legislation of prostitution in Amsterdam.

In the same way, it will be possible to observe the presence of numerous Coffee Shops, which will give rise to talk about the regulation of drugs such as marijuana and mushrooms, as well as the current environment around these issues.

Qué está incluido

Professional guide fluent in English

Información adicional
Cancelaciones

description:
Amsterdam is a vibrant city, best experienced onboard a Pure Boats afternoon cruise. Our afternoon cruises are often booked out, and for good reason! Join us for a relaxing private cruise, easily booked via our online system. We do suggest a 2 hour cruise to really enjoy the relaxing feeling of being on the canals!

name: Beginner Crochet Workshop: Bag
description:
Have you always wanted to learn to crochet, but never got around to it? Then join our fun course in which you can master the basics of crochet in 2 lessons of 2 hours! Start at the very beginning and crochet your very first stitches. What's great about this course is that you not only learn the techniques, but also make a final product: a shoulder bag. This way you'll learn different techniques and stitches and also work towards a final product. This course will prepare you to work on your own projects at home. Material & crochet hook can be included or excluded in your booking - you can always add materials when you arrive for your lesson. We have a selection of colours of 100% recycled t-shirt yarn for you to choose from.

What will you learn?

How to start your work, chain stitch, slip stitch, single crochet stitch, and finishing.

Important to Know

Celebrate the beautiful city of Amsterdam with the wonderful art of embroidery! In this workshop, you'll learn four basic embroidery stitches and create a Dutch inspired artwork as an ode to this inspiring city. This may even be a nice gift for friends visiting from abroad!

The workshop includes embroidery tools and materials, as well as a pattern in case you don't finish your work during the workshop.

name: Shared Amsterdam Light Festival Cruise with Hot Chocolate and Stroopwafel
description:
Experience the enchantment of the Amsterdam Light Festival on an electric boat cruise with beer, wine, glühwein, hot chocolate, and soda. Embark on a journey of wonder and illumination while under warm blankets. Learn about the history of the canals and facts about the city.

Settle into the luxurious comfort of a modern, fully electric boat, designed to shield you from the elements, and set out on a cruise through the vibrant heart of Amsterdam. As twilight descends, pass by a captivating gallery of 20 luminous art installations that adorn the canals.

Wrapped in warmth with provided blankets, immerse yourself in the city's beauty and allure from the cozy haven of your boat. Listen to stories shared by your skipper or host.

Delve into the stories behind the art pieces, explore the rich history of the canals, and uncover insights about the city itself. All the while, indulge in a selection of alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, generously provided without limit from the onboard bar.

The thirteenth edition of Amsterdam Light Festival takes place from 27 November 2025 until 18 January 2026.

What's included

Cruise by electric boat
Local host
Unlimited beer, glühwein, hot chocolate, and soft drink

name: Aruba: Snorkling Adventure Cruise
description:
Stap aan boord bij Flagship Aruba en beleef een onvergetelijke snorkelervaring op het kristalheldere water rondom Aruba. Tijdens deze ontspannen cruise nemen we je mee naar de mooiste snork/spots van het eiland, waaronder kleurrijke koraalriffen, tropische vissen en – als hoogtepunt – het beroemde Antilla scheepswrak, één van de grootste en meest indrukwekkende wrakken in het Caribisch gebied.

Onze crew staat bekend om hun warmte, energie en persoonlijke service. Terwijl jij geniet van het uitzicht, zorgen wij voor professionele snorkeluitrusting, verfrissende drankjes en een relaxte sfeer aan boord. Of je nu een ervaren snorkelaar bent of voor het eerst het water in gaat: wij begeleiden je stap voor stap.

Wat kun je verwachten?

Bezoek aan iconische snorkelplekken zoals Boca Catalina & de Antilla
Snorkeluitrusting inbegrepen
Kleine groepen voor een comfortabele en persoonlijke ervaring
Caribische muziek, open bar & goede vibes
Perfect voor families, stellen en vriendengroepen

Laat je betoveren door de magie van de onderwaterwereld en maak herinneringen die je nooit meer vergeet. Boek jouw Flagship Aruba Snorkel Cruise en ontdek waarom Aruba bekend staat

Embark on a delightful private tour that takes you through the charming Dutch countryside. We’ll stroll through picturesque fishing villages and even visit a working windmill. Immerse yourself in the tranquil atmosphere of this UNESCO World Heritage site, where you’ll witness the beauty of ribbons of farmland, lakes, dikes, and dams. At the Dutch heritage site of Zaanse Schans, you’ll gain insights into how the Golden Age shaped Amsterdam’s urban development. Witness fascinating demonstrations of traditional manufacturing processes and visit a charming wooden shoe factory.
